# RecurringTransaction Feature Shadow Spec

> Recurring Transaction Management - Scheduled income/expense automation with monthly patterns

**Status:** Planned
**Generated by:** spec-lead + @pseudo-worker

---

```sudolang
import("specs/ARCHITECTURE.md")
import("specs/DB_DESIGN.md")
import("specs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md")
import("specs/FEATURES.md")
import("specs/DEPENDENCIES.md")
```

---

## Architecture

```sudolang
// Package-by-Feature Structure
// src/commonMain/kotlin/com/budgetcalendar/features/recurring/
// ├── data/
// │   ├── local/           # SQLDelight queries
// │   ├── repository/      # RecurringTransactionRepositoryImpl
// │   └── mapper/          # RecurringTransactionMapper
// ├── domain/
// │   ├── model/           # RecurringTransaction, RecurrenceType
// │   ├── repository/      # IRecurringTransactionRepository
// │   └── usecase/        # CreateRecurring, GenerateTransactions, ToggleActive
// └── presentation/
//     ├── RecurringViewModel.kt
//     └── RecurringUiState.kt
```

---

## Data Model

```sudolang
// Domain Models
interface RecurringTransaction {
  id: Long;
  accountId: Long;
  destinationAccountId: Long?;    // For TRANSFER type
  amount: Long;                   // In cents (positive)
  dayOfMonth: Int;               // 1-31
  type: RecurrenceType;
  description: String?;
  isActive: Boolean;
  createdAt: Long;
  updatedAt: Long;
}

enum RecurrenceType {
  INCOME = "income"
  EXPENSE = "expense"
  TRANSFER = "transfer"
}

interface CreateRecurringTransactionRequest {
  accountId: Long;
  destinationAccountId: Long?;
  amount: Long;
  dayOfMonth: Int;
  type: RecurrenceType;
  description: String?;
}

interface UpdateRecurringTransactionRequest {
  accountId: Long?;
  destinationAccountId: Long?;
  amount: Long?;
  dayOfMonth: Int?;
  type: RecurrenceType?;
  description: String?;
  isActive: Boolean?;
}

// UI State Models
interface RecurringUiState {
  recurringTransactions: List<RecurringTransaction>;
  selectedRecurring: RecurringTransaction?;
  isLoading: Boolean;
  error: String?;
  upcomingGenerated: List<GeneratedTransaction>;
}

interface GeneratedTransaction {
  recurringId: Long;
  date: Long;
  amount: Long;
  type: RecurrenceType;
  accountId: Long;
  willGenerate: Boolean;
}
```

---

## Constraints

```sudolang
Constraint: RecurringTransaction amount must be > 0; type determines direction
Constraint: dayOfMonth must be between 1 and 31
Constraint: Recurrence type must be one of: INCOME, EXPENSE, TRANSFER
Constraint: TRANSFER type requires destinationAccountId
Constraint: Active recurring transactions generate pending transactions monthly
Constraint: Inactive recurring transactions do not generate transactions
Constraint: Generated transactions have status PENDING by default
Constraint: Deleting recurring transaction does not affect already-generated transactions
Constraint: Description optional, max 200 characters
Constraint: Account deletion cascades to delete recurring transactions
```

---

## Data Flow

```sudolang
// Create Recurring Transaction Flow
User Input |> validateRecurringInput |> CreateRecurringUseCase.execute()
  |> RecurringTransactionRepository.insert()
  |> If (isActive == true) |> GenerateUpcomingTransactions
  |> Return RecurringTransaction
  |> Update RecurringUiState

// Generate Monthly Transactions Flow (Daily Job)
Trigger: Daily at midnight
  |> GetActiveRecurringTransactions
    |> RecurringTransactionRepository.getActive()
  |> ForEach recurring
    |> CalculateNextOccurrence(dayOfMonth)
    |> Check if transaction already exists for this month
    |> If not exists |> CreateTransaction(status=PENDING)
  |> Return List<GeneratedTransaction>

// Toggle Active/Inactive Flow
User Toggle |> UpdateRecurringUseCase.execute(id, isActive)
  |> RecurringTransactionRepository.updateActive()
  |> If (deactivating) |> Keep existing generated transactions
  |> If (activating) |> Generate upcoming transactions
  |> Return Updated RecurringTransaction

// Get Upcoming Generated Flow
User Request |> GetUpcomingGeneratedUseCase.execute(monthsAhead: Int)
  |> GetActiveRecurringTransactions
  |> ForEach |> Calculate occurrences for next N months
  |> Check which ones already have transactions
  |> Return List<GeneratedTransaction> with willGenerate flag

// Delete Recurring Transaction Flow
User Delete |> ConfirmDialog
  |> RecurringTransactionRepository.delete()
  |> Existing generated transactions remain unchanged
  |> Return Success
```

---

## Function Signatures

```sudolang
fn CreateRecurringTransaction(ctx: Context, req: CreateRecurringTransactionRequest): Result<RecurringTransaction>
fn GetRecurringTransactions(ctx: Context): Flow<List<RecurringTransaction>>
fn GetActiveRecurringTransactions(ctx: Context): Flow<List<RecurringTransaction>>
fn GetRecurringTransactionById(ctx: Context, id: Long): Result<RecurringTransaction>
fn GetRecurringTransactionsByAccount(ctx: Context, accountId: Long): Flow<List<RecurringTransaction>>
fn UpdateRecurringTransaction(ctx: Context, id: Long, req: UpdateRecurringTransactionRequest): Result<RecurringTransaction>
fn ToggleRecurringActive(ctx: Context, id: Long, isActive: Boolean): Result<RecurringTransaction>
fn DeleteRecurringTransaction(ctx: Context, id: Long): Result<void>
fn GenerateMonthlyTransactions(ctx: Context): Result<List<Transaction>>
fn GetUpcomingGeneratedTransactions(ctx: Context, monthsAhead: Int): Flow<List<GeneratedTransaction>>
```

```sudolang
// RecurringListItem Props
interface RecurringListItemProps {
  recurring: RecurringTransaction;
  onTap: () -> Unit;
  onToggleActive: (Boolean) -> Void;
  onDelete: () -> Void;
}

// RecurringForm Props
interface RecurringFormProps {
  initialData: RecurringTransaction?;
  accounts: List<Account>;
  onSave: (CreateRecurringTransactionRequest) -> Unit;
  onCancel: () -> Unit;
  onDelete: (RecurringTransaction) -> Void?;
}

// UpcomingGeneratedList Props
interface UpcomingGeneratedListProps {
  generated: List<GeneratedTransaction>;
  onSkip: (GeneratedTransaction) -> Void;
}

// RecurringCard Props
interface RecurringCardProps {
  recurring: RecurringTransaction;
  nextGeneratedDate: Long?;
  monthlyAmount: Long;
  onTap: () -> Void;
}

// Visual: Recurring Indicator
// Active: Green dot (#4CAF50)
// Inactive: Gray dot (#9E9E9E)
// Type icon: income (arrow down green), expense (arrow up red), transfer (swap arrows)
```

---

## Database Schema

```sudolang
table recurring_transactions {
  id: Long PK AUTOINCREMENT
  account_id: Long NOT NULL REFERENCES accounts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
  destination_account_id: Long REFERENCES accounts(id)
  amount: Integer NOT NULL
  day_of_month: Integer NOT NULL CHECK(day_of_month >= 1 AND day_of_month <= 31)
  type: Text NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('income','expense','transfer'))
  description: Text
  is_active: Integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
  created_at: Integer NOT NULL
  updated_at: Integer NOT NULL
}

index idx_recurring_active ON recurring_transactions(is_active)
index idx_recurring_day ON recurring_transactions(day_of_month)
index idx_recurring_account ON recurring_transactions(account_id)
```

---

## SQLDelight Queries

```sudolang
// getAllRecurringTransactions :many
SELECT * FROM recurring_transactions ORDER BY day_of_month;

// getActiveRecurringTransactions :many
SELECT * FROM recurring_transactions WHERE is_active = 1 ORDER BY day_of_month;

// getRecurringTransactionById :one
SELECT * FROM recurring_transactions WHERE id = :id;

// getRecurringTransactionsByAccount :many
SELECT * FROM recurring_transactions WHERE account_id = :account_id ORDER BY day_of_month;

// insertRecurringTransaction :one
INSERT INTO recurring_transactions (account_id, destination_account_id, amount, day_of_month, type, description, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
VALUES (:account_id, :destination_account_id, :amount, :day_of_month, :type, :description, :is_active, :created_at, :updated_at)
RETURNING *;

// updateRecurringTransaction :one
UPDATE recurring_transactions 
SET account_id = :account_id, destination_account_id = :destination_account_id, amount = :amount, 
    day_of_month = :day_of_month, type = :type, description = :description, 
    is_active = :is_active, updated_at = :updated_at
WHERE id = :id
RETURNING *;

// toggleRecurringActive :one
UPDATE recurring_transactions 
SET is_active = :is_active, updated_at = :updated_at
WHERE id = :id
RETURNING *;

// deleteRecurringTransaction :exec
DELETE FROM recurring_transactions WHERE id = :id;
```

## Dependencies

```sudolang
// External Dependencies
Dependency: sqldelight (2.0.1) - Database
Dependency: koin (3.5.3) - DI
Dependency: kotlinx-coroutines (1.7.3) - Async
Dependency: kotlinx-datetime (0.5.0) - Date handling

// Internal Dependencies
Dependency: core/database - SQLDelight queries
Dependency: features/accounts - Account ID foreign key, account existence validation
Dependency: features/transactions - Generate Transaction entities with PENDING status
Dependency: features/budget - Safe to Spend affected by generated pending transactions
Dependency: features/calendar - Recurring indicators on calendar days
Dependency: features/sandbox - Recurring transactions do not apply to sandbox
```

---

## Edge Cases

| Case | Condition | Handling |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Day 29-31 on short months | dayOfMonth > daysInMonth | Generate on last day of month (28/29/30/31) |
| Account deleted | account_id foreign key | Cascade delete recurring transactions |
| Transfer to same account | source_account == destination_account | Return error: "Cannot transfer to the same account" |
| Recurring already generated this month | Transaction exists for this month+recurringId | Skip generation, mark willGenerate=false |
| Toggle inactive mid-month | Deactivate with pending generated | Keep existing pending transactions |
| Reactivate after deactivation | Activate with existing transactions | Generate future months only |
| Delete recurring with pending | Pending transaction from this recurring exists | Confirm deletion, pending remains |
| Change account on existing recurring | Update account_id | Future generations use new account |
| Zero amount recurring | amount <= 0 | Return error: "Amount must be greater than zero" |
| Multiple recurrences same day | dayOfMonth matches multiple | Generate all, order by created_at |

---

## Integration Notes

```sudolang
// Calendar Integration
// - Calendar displays recurring indicators on days with active recurrences
// - Tapping day shows generated pending transactions
// - Different icon for income vs expense recurrences

// Safe to Spend Integration
// - Generated PENDING transactions affect Safe to Spend immediately
// - Recurring INCOME increases Safe to Spend (adds amount)
// - Recurring EXPENSE decreases Safe to Spend (subtracts amount)
// - Recurring TRANSFER: source deducts, destination adds

// Sandbox Integration
// - Recurring transactions do NOT auto-generate in sandbox
// - User can manually add recurring to sandbox if desired
// - Sandbox shows "Real recurrences" indicator
```
